pi_items.source: Confirmed in task.json description line 5, setup_task.sh lines 10-11, and export_result.sh line 10. All scripts consistently reference this exact path.
Confirmed in task.json description line 5, setup_task.sh lines 11, 19, and export_result.sh line 11. All scripts consistently reference this exact path.

Confirmed in task.json metadata, verifier.py line 29 (used as default), and used in verification checks on lines 68 and 74. The value is reasonable for a complete cranial bone surface mesh - research indicates cranial meshes typically contain 35,000-500,000 triangles depending on segmentation detail. Cross-referenced with export_skull_stl task which uses min_triangle_count of 10,000 for baseline requirement, suggesting 50,000 is plausible for a more complex mesh with better detail.

pi_items.verified_value: Not used in verification logic (unverified through code review)
pi_items.source: Code analysis: The metadata defines min_volume_mm3 at task.json line 20, but grep search of verifier.py shows this value is never extracted from metadata. The verifier.py line 106 checks hardcoded threshold: 'if vol > 1000 and area > 1000:' without using the min_volume_mm3 metadata value. This is a contradiction between metadata definition and verification implementation.

Confirmed in setup_invesalius.sh lines 71-73: download URL 'https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip' contains the '0051' dataset identifier. setup_task.sh line 8 references '/home/ga/DICOM/ct_cranium' as the location. This is the official InVesalius sample dataset, confirmed also in export_skull_stl task's validated_pi.json.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires reporting mesh statistics (triangle count, volume, surface area) of a cranial bone surface generated from the official InVesalius CT Cranium sample dataset (case ID 0051), which is downloaded from https://github.com/invesalius/invesalius3/releases/download/v3.0/0051.zip. The bone segmentation uses the standard Bone preset (226-3071 HU range) to generate a 3D surface mesh. The task expects the STL output path to be /home/ga/Documents/skull.stl with a minimum of 50,000 triangles for a complete cranial mesh. The report is saved to /home/ga/Documents/mesh_stats.txt. The metadata specifies a minimum volume of 50,000 mm³, but this threshold is not actually enforced by the verification logic - the verifier only checks that volume and surface area values are non-zero and greater than 1000.
